summary: QA validation of CR A-1 (Channel Filter) on Scan & Order customer menu. 8/8 cases verified — 36/36 helper unit assertions pass; 5/5 mocked-API menu-render scenarios pass; search filter, ADD-button gate (incl. legacy permissive default), and cart-on-channel-switch confirm UX (cancel/accept/empty-cart paths) all pass; PreviousOrderItems regression confirmed via static analysis; out-of-scope file scope confirmed via git diff. Verdict: PASS.

case_1_helper_isItemAllowedForChannel.evidence: 36/36 assertions in /app/frontend/src/utils/__tests__/channelEligibility.test.cjs cover 6 item shapes (allYes, dineinOnly, deliveryOnly, takeawayOnly, legacy-no-flags, allNo) x 4 channels (dinein, takeaway, take_away, delivery) plus null/empty/unknown orderType edges and null/undefined item edges. take_away aliased to takeaway. Legacy items with no flags PERMISSIVE on every channel (matches D-1 default). null orderType returns true. null item returns false.

case_1b_helper_getChannelLabel.evidence: dinein->'Dine-in', takeaway->'Takeaway', take_away->'Takeaway', delivery->'Delivery', null/'curbside'->'this order type'.

case_2_menu_render_filter.evidence: All 5 scan-URL scenarios produced expected visible/hidden item sets: /478?type=walkin&orderType=dinein -> [AllAllowed, DineinOnly]; orderType=delivery -> [AllAllowed, DeliveryOnly]; orderType=takeaway -> [AllAllowed, TakeawayOnly]; orderType=take_away (alias) -> [AllAllowed, TakeawayOnly]; type=room&orderType=dinein -> [AllAllowed, DineinOnly]. Mocked POST /web/restaurant-product via Playwright route. Empty-after-filter category does not render an empty card (only one category in test set, but search-empty path Case 3 confirms downstream filters operate on already-filtered set).

case_3_search_filter.evidence: On dinein flow, search='DeliveryOnly' returns 0 results (channel filter applied first). search='AllAllowed' returns 1 result.

case_4_add_button_gate.evidence: Dinein menu shows 3 ADD buttons (AllAllowed + DineinOnly + LegacyNoFlags). Delivery menu shows 3 ADD buttons (AllAllowed + DeliveryOnly + LegacyNoFlags); DineinOnly hidden; LegacyNoFlags renders ADD on every channel (regression OK).

case_5_cartcontext_addToCart_guard.verdict: PASS (verified via code inspection + indirect runtime evidence)
case_5_cartcontext_addToCart_guard.evidence: CartContext.js line 219-225 implements `if (activeOrderType && !isItemAllowedForChannel(item, activeOrderType)) { toast.error(...); logger.cart(...); return; }` BEFORE setCart mutation. Signature is `(item, variations=[], add_ons=[], activeOrderType=null)`, so legacy 3-arg callers are byte-identical (default null bypasses the guard). MenuItems.jsx now passes scannedOrderType as 4th arg from all 3 call-sites (handleAddToCart, handleAddToCartFromModal, handleIncrement). Indirect runtime evidence: in Case 6 takeaway flow, addToCart accepted both AllAllowed (allowed for takeaway) and TakeawayOnly (allowed for takeaway), confirming legitimate adds still work via the new 4-arg path.

case_6_channel_switch_prompt.evidence:
Cancel path: window.confirm called once with exact text 'Switching to Delivery will remove 1 item(s) not available for Delivery:

TakeawayOnly

Continue?' — cart unchanged (still contained TakeawayOnly + AllAllowed). Confirm path: TakeawayOnly removed, AllAllowed retained. Empty-cart switch: 0 confirm calls. All-allowed cart switch: not separately invoked but covered by the disallowed.length===0 branch which returns no prompt (same code path as empty-cart).

case_7_edit_order_regression.evidence: Channel filter operates only on filterItems() in MenuItems.jsx:344 which filters the menu's `items` array. PreviousOrderItems component reads CartContext.previousOrderItems (editOrder?.previousItems) and renders that list independently — no channel filter is applied to it anywhere in the diff. CartContext.addToCart guard (Case 5) DOES apply during edit sessions, so new menu adds during edit are correctly channel-gated as required.

case_8_out_of_scope_diff.evidence: git show HEAD --name-only confirms ONLY these files changed: .gitignore, frontend/src/components/MenuItem/MenuItem.jsx, frontend/src/context/CartContext.js, frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.jsx, frontend/src/pages/MenuItems.jsx, frontend/src/utils/channelEligibility.js (NEW), memory/change_requests/PRODUCT_API_FIELD_MAPPING_A1_STATUS_2026-05-09.md. Confirmed UNTOUCHED: frontend/src/services/orderService.ts, frontend/src/utils/transformers/helpers.js, frontend/src/pages/ReviewOrder.jsx, frontend/src/pages/OrderSuccess.jsx, backend/server.py, frontend/src/services/paymentService.ts, frontend/src/utils/taxCalculation.js, frontend/src/utils/deliveryChargeCalculation.js, frontend/src/hooks/useScannedTable.js, frontend/src/utils/itemAvailability.js, frontend/src/components/OrderModeSelector/OrderModeSelector.jsx.

critical_code_review_comments: isItemAllowedForChannel correctly uses `!== 'No'` semantics so any non-'No' value (incl. null/undefined/'Yes'/'1'/'Y') is allowed — matches D-1 transformer permissive defaults. No risk of false negatives from missing flags.

addToCart 4th-arg `activeOrderType` defaults to null which bypasses the guard — preserves backwards compatibility for any legacy 3-arg caller that may exist elsewhere (e.g., test code, future callers). Good defensive design.

LandingPage handleModeChange correctly orders side-effects: confirm gate -> early return on cancel -> remove disallowed items -> setSelectedMode -> updateOrderType. No race where mode changes before cart cleanup.
Confirm dialog text uses up to 5 names with 'and N more' suffix — UX requirement met.
Out-of-scope assertion verified: PreviousOrderItems, orderService, payment, tax, KOT, payload paths all UNTOUCHED in HEAD diff.

Minor (non-blocking): handleModeChange uses native window.confirm() rather than a styled modal. This works on all platforms and matches CR scope, but a future polish CR could replace it with the project's existing modal/dialog component for consistency. Out of scope for A-1.

Pre-existing console errors observed during testing ('[MENU] Failed to fetch restaurant config' from a config endpoint returning HTML) are UNRELATED to A-1; they exist on the preprod env and were untouched by this CR.
seed_data_creation: Mocked Product API responses via Playwright route interception with 5 synthetic items (AllAllowed, DineinOnly, DeliveryOnly, TakeawayOnly, LegacyNoFlags). No real seed data written.
test_credentials: Not required — A-1 operates on the public/pre-login menu surface.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All A-1 acceptance assertions pass. If any post-merge regression appears: (a) re-run `node /app/frontend/src/utils/__tests__/channelEligibility.test.cjs` for helper sanity; (b) re-run the Playwright route-mock script for menu render filter; (c) PreviousOrderItems must NEVER be channel-filtered — that is a deferred decision per the original audit doc.
rca_of_the_issue: No defect found. Implementation correctly addresses the original audit finding (item.dinein/takeaway/delivery flags from D-1 were ignored). A-1 closes the gap with: (1) helper isItemAllowedForChannel + getChannelLabel; (2) page-level filter in MenuItems.filterItems applied BEFORE search/veg/dietary; (3) per-item ADD-button gate in MenuItem.jsx; (4) defensive 4th-arg guard in CartContext.addToCart; (5) cart-aware confirm prompt in LandingPage.handleModeChange. PreviousOrderItems intentionally left unfiltered per owner decision.
